# anypoint_api_instance_sla_tier (Resource)

Creates and manages an SLA tier on an API Manager instance.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "anypoint_api_instance_sla_tier" "gold" {
  org_id       = var.org_id
  env_id       = var.env_id
  api_id       = anypoint_apim_mule4.api.id
  name         = "Gold"
  description  = "High throughput tier"
  auto_approve = false
  status       = "ACTIVE"

  limits {
    visible                    = true
    time_period_in_milliseconds = 60000
    maximum_requests           = 1000
  }

  limits {
    visible                    = false
    time_period_in_milliseconds = 3600000
    maximum_requests           = 10000
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `api_id` (String) The API Manager instance id.
- `env_id` (String) The environment id where the resource is defined.
- `limits` (Block List, Min: 1) Rate limit definitions for this tier. At least one must have visible=true. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--limits))
- `name` (String) The SLA tier name.
- `org_id` (String) The organization id where the resource is defined.

### Optional

- `api_version_id` (String) The API version id. Defaults to api_id when not provided.
- `auto_approve` (Boolean) Whether contract requests for this tier are auto-approved.
- `description` (String) The SLA tier description.
- `last_updated` (String) The last time this resource has been updated locally.
- `status` (String) The tier status. One of: ACTIVE, DEPRECATED.

### Read-Only

- `application_count` (Number) The number of contracts bound to this tier.
- `created_at` (String) The date the tier was created.
- `id` (String) The unique composite id of this resource: org_id/env_id/api_id/tier_id.
- `master_organization_id` (String) The root organization id.
- `organization_id` (String) The organization id returned by the platform.
- `tier_id` (Number) The numeric SLA tier id assigned by the platform.
- `updated_at` (String) The date the tier was last updated.

<a id="nestedblock--limits"></a>
### Nested Schema for `limits`

Required:

- `maximum_requests` (Number) The maximum number of requests allowed in the time window.
- `time_period_in_milliseconds` (Number) The time window in milliseconds.
- `visible` (Boolean) Whether this limit is visible to API consumers.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Import an existing SLA tier using the composite id: org_id/env_id/api_id/tier_id
#
# tier_id is the numeric id assigned by Anypoint (visible in the UI URL and API list response).
#
# Post-import behaviour:
#   After import, run `terraform plan`. If api_version_id was not set in config,
#   a planned change may appear because the platform returns it on list but it
#   differs from the default empty string. Set api_version_id = <api_id> in your
#   config to suppress this.
#   Second plan after apply should show "No changes."

terraform import anypoint_api_instance_sla_tier.gold <org_id>/<env_id>/<api_id>/<tier_id>
```
